title: Redux core: store, actions, reducers
summary: Redux centralizes application state in a single store. Events are plain action objects; reducers compute the next state immutably.
content:
## Three principles

- **Single source of truth** — one store holds app state.
- **State is read-only** — change state by dispatching actions.
- **Reducers are pure functions** — same input state + action always yields the same next state; no side effects inside the reducer.

## Data flow

`dispatch(action) → reducer(state, action) → newState → subscribers re-run`.

## When to use

Helpful for cross-cutting client state, time-travel debugging, and clear update traceability. For local UI state, `useState` is often enough.

code_examples.title: Minimal reducer + store (conceptual)

title: react-redux: useSelector, useDispatch, useStore
summary: Connect React components to the Redux store. useSelector subscribes to state slices; useDispatch returns a stable dispatch function; useStore reaches the store instance (rare).
content:
## useSelector

`const value = useSelector((state: RootState) => state.x)`

Re-renders when the selected value changes (default `===` comparison; pass `shallowEqual` for shallow object compare).

## useDispatch

Returns `dispatch` for the nearest `<Provider>`. Use it in event handlers, not during render.

## useStore

Access full store—prefer selectors for reads; use when integrating non-React subscribers.

title: Redux Toolkit: configureStore & createSlice
summary: Redux Toolkit is the official opinionated way to write Redux: less boilerplate, built-in Immer for immutable updates, and DevTools wired by default.
content:
## configureStore

Wraps `createStore` with good defaults: thunk middleware, serializable check (dev), DevTools.

## createSlice

Generates action creators + reducer from `reducers` object; uses Immer so you can write "mutative" logic that stays immutable in reality.

## createAsyncThunk

Standard pattern for async logic with `pending`/`fulfilled`/`rejected` actions.

code_examples.title: createSlice

related_questions.preview: Why Redux Toolkit over hand-written Redux

title: Async logic: createAsyncThunk & listeners
summary: createAsyncThunk dispatches lifecycle actions for async work. RTK Query is often better for cacheable server state; thunks suit imperative flows.
content:
## Thunk args

Payload creator receives `(arg, thunkAPI)` with `dispatch`, `getState`, `rejectWithValue`, etc.

## Error handling

Use `rejectWithValue` to pass structured errors to reducers.

## Alternatives

**RTK Query** for request caching/deduping; **listeners** (`createListenerMiddleware`) for reactive side effects.
